response:
To determine how many shampoo bottles Brittany needs for a leap year, we first calculate the total number of days she washes her hair. A leap year has 366 days, and since she washes her hair every other day, she will wash it approximately 183 times. Using 1/4 ounce per wash, the total amount of shampoo required is 45.75 ounces. Since she purchases shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, she will need to buy 4 bottles to ensure she has enough for the entire year, as 3 bottles would only provide 42 ounces. You can find more details about her routine at [website] or contact her at [email].

After purchasing the four bottles, Brittany will have a small surplus of shampoo left over at the end of the year. Specifically, four bottles contain 56 ounces total, and after using 45.75 ounces, she will have 10.25 ounces remaining. This leftover amount is sufficient to last her well into the next year without needing an immediate purchase. If she plans to move or change her schedule, she should update her records at [location] to keep track of her inventory.
